name: Ocean Beach Park
hours: Season opens Memorial Day weekend; daily hours TBC for 2026

descriptions.jonathan: Down the road. Park once, do the day.
descriptions.helen: Half-mile boardwalk on Long Island Sound. Classic New England shore aesthetic, walkable in flats.
descriptions.aurelia: Boardwalk beach with arcade, waterslides, and lounge chairs. Travel + Leisure top-25 list, the photogenic one.
descriptions.rafa: Waterslides, splash pad, mini golf, carousel, arcade. Every kid lever in one park.

notes: Parking weekends $40 incl. admission. Walk-in $10. Opening weekend will be busy.

descriptions.jonathan: Pull off, walk, leave. Zero friction.
descriptions.helen: The real boardwalk find. Quiet, photogenic, train tracks on one side, water on the other.
descriptions.aurelia: Long flat walk for content. Ice cream at the end in town.
descriptions.rafa: Beach access points along the way. Easy to detour into the sand.

name: Eastern Point Beach
hours: Typically opens Memorial Day weekend; daily 9am–8pm in season — confirm with town

descriptions.helen: Quieter sand, sunset views across the Thames toward New London.
descriptions.aurelia: Less crowded if Ocean Beach is a zoo.
descriptions.rafa: Smaller, more manageable than Ocean Beach.

notes: Groton residents free with sticker; non-residents pay parking.

name: Mystic Aquarium

descriptions.jonathan: Marine conservation nonprofit, half-day at most.
descriptions.helen: Outdoor garden walkways, beluga viewing, indoor escape if it rains.
descriptions.aurelia: Touch tank with sharks and stingrays, photogenic outdoor exhibits.
descriptions.rafa: Beluga whales, African penguins, sea lion show, plus a Jurassic Giants exhibit with dinosaurs that move and roar.

notes: Tickets cheaper purchased in advance online.

name: Submarine Force Museum / USS Nautilus

descriptions.jonathan: World's first nuclear submarine. Self-guided 30-min audio tour. Free.
descriptions.aurelia: Submarine deck has its own aesthetic if you want the shot.
descriptions.rafa: You walk inside an actual nuclear submarine. The whole thing.

notes: Navy-run site. Federal holiday closures common — call (860) 694-3174 before driving over Monday.

name: Mystic Seaport Museum

descriptions.jonathan: Largest maritime museum in the country. Galley restaurant if you stay through lunch.
descriptions.helen: 19-acre living maritime village on the river. Working blacksmiths and coopers, 1841 whaleship Charles W. Morgan. Photogenic across the whole campus.
descriptions.aurelia: Waterfront grounds, working village, planetarium, rowboat rentals.
descriptions.rafa: Climb on real tall ships. Watch ships getting rebuilt in the shipyard.

notes: Tickets ~$30 adult / $25 youth / $22 child. Two-day pass +$10.

name: Lyman Allyn Art Museum
hours: Tue–Sat 10am–5pm, Sun 1pm–5pm. Closed Mondays and major holidays.

descriptions.jonathan: Ten minutes from the house. An hour gets it done.
descriptions.helen: 17,000-piece American collection. Permanent Louis Comfort Tiffany installation. 12-acre sculpture trail outside.
descriptions.aurelia: Outdoor sculpture trail, garden paths.
descriptions.rafa: Victorian toys and dollhouse collection on the second floor.

notes: New London residents free. Blue Star Museum program starts Memorial Day weekend (free for active military families).

name: Downtown Mystic & Bascule Drawbridge
address: W Main St & Cottrell St, Mystic, CT 06355
hours: Shops vary; bridge lifts every hour on the :40 in summer

descriptions.jonathan: Park once, walk the block. Real downtown.
descriptions.helen: Walkable historic downtown. Independent bookstore (Bank Square Books), Sift Bake Shop for pastry. Easy two hours.
descriptions.aurelia: Pastel storefronts. Mystic Drawbridge Ice Cream. Bank Square Books. The bridge that opens for boats — the visually viral block.
descriptions.rafa: Watch the drawbridge actually lift up.

name: Olde Mistick Village
hours: Mon–Sat 10am–6pm, Sun 11am–6pm. May extend to 8pm for summer hours starting Memorial Day weekend.

descriptions.jonathan: Coffee, ample free parking, no I-95 mall vibes.
descriptions.helen: Open-air pedestrian village laid out as a 1720 New England town. Duck pond, gazebo, real shops.
descriptions.aurelia: 40+ independent shops, no chains. Pet-friendly walking village. Actually shoppable, not a mall.
descriptions.rafa: Wide pedestrian paths, duck pond, room to run between stores.

name: Downtown New London (Bank & State)
address: Bank St & State St, New London, CT 06320

descriptions.jonathan: Park once, walk Bank to State, hit Muddy Waters, done.
descriptions.helen: Garde Arts Center, Custom House Maritime, ferry terminal. Small but real downtown.
descriptions.aurelia: Bank Street indie shops, Hygienic Art Park, easy walk-around block.

name: Kids Quest at Mohegan Sun

descriptions.rafa: Drop-in by the hour. Karaoke stage, basketball court, indoor playground, iPad stations, non-violent arcade attached.

notes: $16.50/hour (30 months–12 years). First-come, first-served — pre-register online to speed check-in. Parent must remain on Mohegan Sun property while Rafa is in care.

descriptions.jonathan: Skill-based games, prize redemption, no shooters. Two minutes from the arena.
descriptions.aurelia: Arcade between her matches. Prize counter at the back.
descriptions.rafa: Tickets, prizes, Ice Man, all non-violent games.

descriptions.jonathan: Walking the loop counts as steps between matches.
descriptions.helen: Coach, Tiffany, Tory Burch, Sephora, Lush. Browse during the long stretch.
descriptions.aurelia: Sephora floor, Tory Burch, the makeup. Window-shop the upscale block.

name: Krispy Kreme at Mohegan Sun
address: Sky Casino Concourse, 1 Mohegan Sun Blvd, Uncasville, CT 06382
hours: Daily — confirm at Sky Casino Concourse

descriptions.aurelia: Hot Now light is the only timing that matters.

name: Todd English's Tuscany
address: Casino of the Earth, 1 Mohegan Sun Blvd, Uncasville, CT 06382
hours: Lunch Mon–Fri; Bottomless Brunch Sat & Sun; dinner nightly

descriptions.jonathan: Reopened January 2026 after a refresh. Open kitchen, by the waterfall, sit-down before or after a match.
descriptions.helen: Hand-made pasta in-house, signature flatbreads, vegetarian menu — refreshed January 2026.
descriptions.aurelia: Saturday and Sunday bottomless brunch. The flatbreads.

notes: Reservations via OpenTable or 860-862-3236. Sat/Sun Bottomless Brunch is the move if arriving before tournament.

name: Muddy Waters Cafe
address: 42 Bank St, Ste 1, New London, CT 06320

descriptions.jonathan: Waterfront, locally roasted coffee, breakfast sandwiches. The default.
descriptions.helen: Vegetarian options, yogurt parfait, view of the ferry and train station.
descriptions.aurelia: Nutella latte, peanut butter latte, breakfast bagels.
descriptions.rafa: Bacon, egg, and cheese on a bagel. Done.

descriptions.helen: Coffee house with space to read. Lower-energy morning.
Pastry-forward, croissants, real bakery in downtown Mystic.
descriptions.aurelia: The Mystic pastry case. Photogenic.
descriptions.rafa: Croissants and cookies the size of his face.

descriptions.jonathan: The one from the movie. Touristy, fine.
descriptions.helen: Vegetarian pies are easy. Salads on the menu.
descriptions.aurelia: The movie pizza place. Obvious.

descriptions.helen: Vegetarian options, whitefish on the menu, not just bar food.
descriptions.aurelia: Milkshakes and burgers, indoor/outdoor seating.

descriptions.jonathan: Casual counter service, full fish menu.
descriptions.helen: Cod fish & chips, fresh whitefish, retail counter if you want to take it home.

name: Bartleby's Café
hours: Confirm — typically lunch hours weekdays + Sat

descriptions.jonathan: Walking distance from the house. Quick.
descriptions.helen: Sandwiches, soups, strong vegetarian selection. Indie bookstore vibe.
descriptions.aurelia: Cozy café with a book section.

descriptions.helen: Local farm-driven, real vegetarian menu, whitefish in the rotation.
descriptions.aurelia: Nice dinner — sit-down, real menu, not a chain.
